txt: 'Customs' like complete disregard for the *Rule of Law*?
*Detective*.
*Everyone* knows how to do that! You're a special man. You're going to point it at *yourself*. Go ahead, show them.
*I want to point it at them*, phah! Quit your whining.
*Nothing*. You better hope he doesn't abuse his authority. There's *a lot* of it.
*Serious* question time. This man is no innocent. No one is.
*Why* did you say that? Did you forget you're a cop... again?
...*owe* him for? This is a trap.
A compliment from Lieutenant Kitsuragi! Not bad.
A glib remark. Don't let it stand -- assert yourself.
A leader like Titus doesn't let one of his guys out of sight easily -- especially at a time like this.
A man like Titus is way too confident to get that analogy.
A motive. A *real* cop gets a motive now.
A patrol officer is the lowest rank in the RCM, below lieutenant and sergeant.
A respectful nod. You proved something here.
A terrible shame, still within him. The lobes of his ears are red with it, the shame and smallness of what he became.
A very minor victory.
AUTHORITY
About now he is ready to believe in anything.
Accusations of corruption. Push them aside with a sharp change of topic, officer! Put the heat on *him*.
Again? Seriously?
Ah... ahhh. You continue to display such humility. This is to be admired! Refusing praise for your personal integrity is most honourable indeed.
Already did that. You were too *proud* to take the check.
Also, it has to be said -- this man would make a good RCM sergeant. Or maybe even lieutenant. Just don't let him anywhere near women.
Alternative translation: Don't even *think* you can drive my MC.
An appropriate warning to those who'd cross the line and pull the city into madness once more...
And they're all well motivated. At least the ones you've seen.
And when a police officer asks -- you must answer.
And you? Is it an honour to work with you? Don't ask him, ask yourself.
Another dead body. This is your job. Steel yourself.
Are you going to let a semi-conscious degenerate disrespect you?
Are you ready to drink deep from the cup of honour?
Artists, yet again!! Harassing citizens, stealing badges, 'occupying' public spaces with 'installations' like the one here...
As Lieutenant Kitsuragi explained, a patrol officer is the lowest rank in the RCM, below lieutenant and sergeant.
As if he recalled that he's, in fact, a decorated police lieutenant, and not a naughty boy.
As you look around this room full of sweaty men, swearing, drinking, spitting out tobacco.... does this look familiar?
Ask him again.
At last, a book gives you the respect and validation you're entitled to.
Backing up was a bad idea, now he thinks he's established dominance over you.
Be untroubled. If anything, this small moment has made him respect you more.
Before it's too late, please.
Bring him to heel.
But it won't be this one.
But she *knows* you're police.
Citizens must be warned. This is not only a declaration of the dead but a warning for those still alive. It comes, indomitably.
Coach Physical Instrument is overdoing it -- even by my standards. Say something else first.
Congratulations, Honourable One! You have become a member of a noble faculty. A man kneels, an Honour Cop rises...
Cracked it. All in a good day's work.
Crackling static... more static and yes, more of that sweet static. The violence of your words is obviously scaring her.
Cuno is a merciful king. Kind to his wayward subjects.
DEFEND YOURSELF. Immediately. They're laughing at you.
Damn it, I was really hoping it would be a Cop World. Okay, well, let's see...
Damn right. If he keeps mouthing off, you should take his pants too.
Dick Mullen won't be sent to the clink for the sake of some *legal niceties*!
Did you already try the gun thing? I hear the gun thing is excellent, and has great results.
Do *not* go along with this.

Do marriages make any sense? Does honour? You're not a filthy philosopher, you're an officer of law. It's time to *ace this case* and not brood over your reputation. We can deal with the perception management later.
Do you hear that contempt in his voice? He's always considered himself *above* you.
Does it mean she's *unlawfully* occupying a part of the house? Shameful!
Don't arrest her. Push her a bit more but then let her off. That Ruby theory was solid. (And she's beautiful.)
Don't back down now. Say what you said again -- *proudly*.
Don't be fooled. He's watching you to make sure you don't act unprofessionally.
Don't concern yourself with the feelings of a LAWBREAKER.
Don't give him the pleasure.
Don't let him drink that. One more push, quick!
Don't say anything, size him up first.
Don't think about it too much! Just say it -- are you ready to get knee-deep in honour?
Dumbo, do something, you can't zone out now, this isn't doodle-loo dance time.
Eating this pie will almost certainly make you less of a man. What were you thinking, agreeing to take a wheat-free, meat-free pie from that sleazy bastard?
Eclectic taste? This shop is full of *stolen* goods -- a blight on the community!
Embarrassing. You should know what a misconduct fine is by now. Make it up by being especially *unkind* to him in future dealings.
Enough, officer. This begging is below your dignity.
Establish authority, yes! AUTHORITY! Feverish thoughts race through your mind.
Even among the honourable only the most dignified are deemed worthy -- but you, oh honourable one, have excelled... in honour.
Even better! It's like a shakedown, but somehow legal. This is "civil asset forfeiture."
Even if she didn't write it, she should CLEAN IT.
Even now, Kim is a paragon of professionalism: he's trying to make a clean cut of telling her she was betrayed.
Everyone can see you picked some wrong options. Next time you have to come up with better things to say.
Exactly -- but what?
Exactly!
Explosive laughter follows. To his men, Titus Hardie is a golden god. They want to laugh at his jokes even before they leave his lips. This guy is a born leader.
Failure to aid a police investigation.
Fear of the truth.
Feels like success.
Filthy narco! And look -- it's made right here in Revachol, by Saint-Batiste. A disgraceful pharmaceutical company attempting to lure people into the gutter. You should destroy it!
Finally someone who understands the plight of narcomania! Play it cool, you might have an ally here.
Finally, compliance.
For a moment frozen in time this old soldier is your superior officer, inspiring you to be a better man. It feels good. Comforting.
For a moment, the Cafeteria Manager fidgets under the lieutenant's gaze. Then he gives in.
For a moment, the man fidgets under the lieutenant's gaze. Then he gives in.
For the eighty thousand under your jurisdiction.
Forget about that for a moment. You need to address that remark first.
From the bottom of your blue soul, a term emerges:
Garbage!
Generally speaking, it would be *dishonourable* to take it. But then... it looks like a military uniform! And wearing that is very honourable. So, a conflict of honour. It's up to you.
Go forth and commit even more honourable deeds. And should your resolve ever waiver, remember The Rite of Honour. It will always cleanse a man's soul...
Goddamn right something's the matter! Lieutenant Eyebrow here thinks he can shut you down like some brow-less freak? Thinks you're just going to lie back and take it?!
Good man! You didn't cheat the poor drunk out of his money. It's very *honourable* of you.
Good. A power move. *Purposefully* concentrate on something else first.
Good. Now put it in your mouth.
Good. That was a lie most honourable. Sometimes we must hide the truth from those who wouldn't understand it. The Less-Honoured Ones.
Good. You feel its presence. Now you are ready for the *Rite of Honour*.
He *has* authority in the group, but it wasn't him calling the shots that night. Hasn't been him for a long time.
He could say he was taking the fall for someone. It's useless in court without a motive, go be a cop now, don't mess it up...
He couldn't take it like you. It irks him -- then he gets over it.
He didn't answer *your* question.
He does not want to make it feel like you knowing it is some big deal.
He doesn't let it show, but must be a little impressed. You've put a lot of things together, fast.
He doesn't want to, but if there is one more cryptozoological run-around, he *must* force the investigation back on track. This better be it...
He doesn't want to. But he must.
He has no respect for you personally, but this man sees himself as a law-abiding citizen -- and you a representative of that law. He tries to avoid outright conflict.
He has no respect for you personally, but this man sees himself as a law-abiding citizen -- and you a representative of that law.
He hates the Union, but grudgingly recognizes its power over him -- so he's directing his frustration at you instead. Retaliate!
He is facing overwhelmingly superior firepower -- and he knows it.
He is not used to commanding, or leading. He feels uncomfortable. He'd rather shoot to kill...
He is restraining himself from using a parental tone with you right now.
He knows Eugene is more lieutenant material than he is.
He knows it's something. He's just not ready to say you knew more about Ruby than he does -- yet.
He means *very* little.
He means it. He doesn't want to be the Ice Bear Cop.
He means it. The RCM and its enemies will not be discussed on this coast.
He really doesn't like you ruffling their feathers like that -- on what might be the eve of battle.
He really takes great pride in being in Evrart's good graces.
He seems to be following his orders well enough for now -- but beneath it all there is a boiling rage. And a dangerous carelessness.
He shrinks back a bit under the lieutenant's severe gaze.
He thinks it weak to renege on the courtesy he already decided to pay you. Moral superiority is important to him.
He thinks of apologizing but decides against it.
He trusts you have plenty of things in the task chain, lined up.
He trusts your gut feeling on this.
He wants to send a message: "Even the police are working for me."
He was exaggerating. People blame *cops* for everything that goes wrong in the world. This has nothing to do with you.
He wasn't calling the shots that night... or any night. If anything, he was used as a tool -- for carrying, or clean-up.
He won't, though, because he's nothing but a sissy *bureaucrat*.
He's asserting his leadership talents because he is concerned about you.
He's been expecting this. He's really puffed himself up.
He's had enough. He's really gonna push their buttons now.
He's letting his pride get in the way.
He's not gonna give you money, what are you doing? Clearly you were just profiling.
He's not judging you. He's respectfully acknowledging the difficulty of working in your precinct.
He's not leaving you a lot of room to back off from this.
He's so proud of himself, you're tempted to believe that *he* just defused the situation.
He's so sure it's him -- but it's not that simple. There's someone above him (or beside him?) sharing the leadership. Hard to say who...
He's tired and weary and you are better not to test his patience.
He's truly satisfied with your performance in Death Island right now.
He's trying to throw you off your game with this *Du Bois* nonsense -- don't give him the pleasure.
He's trying to throw you off your game, thinking you don't know your name -- but you do! So the joke's on him, ha ha!
He's under orders. And orders -- are orders.
He's used to giving orders and having them obeyed immediately. You should not indulge him.
He's used to knowing who to trust. This makes him uncomfortable.
He's watching his old man get the big prize at the claw game.
Hear that? He wants you to say things. Say one!
Hear that? Set justice on its feet. CONFRONT EVRART.
Here is a man who has had more than his fair share of heated arguments -- and would, surprising as it may seem, prefer not to have any more.
Here we go again! "Lost gun, lost gun" -- trying to terrorize you!
Here's a former junkie. I can recognize one when I see it.
Hey, maybe it's your paperwork, officer?
Hey, what is this funeral vibe here? You did what any good cop would do. Pat yourself on the back and move on.
His father taught him *respect*.
His patience has all but run out. The boy better wrap it up...
His tone is like that of a drill-sergeant addressing new recruits.
His tone is so solemn that if your eyes were closed, you could swear he has taken a knee.
His tone lacks its usual intensity, like he doesn't feel he has the right to speak on the matter.
His tone leaves no room for arguments. Giving orders is a second skin to the old carabineer.
His voice is low, but firm. All she can say is...
Honour comes from deep within you and to truly know oneself, to *know* one's honour, one must reach deep within oneself and *touch* one's honour...
Honourable One, you have accumulated a substantial amount of honour points on your life-path. You are a man most virtuous.
How did you not know that?! Aren't you a *cop*? You're leaving a weak impression here, say something sure handed!
How is that sign cleanup going, huh? Fuck the police and all that? She said she would clean it.
How many times must I tell you...
I saw what you were thinking. You want to say 'what are you going to *do* to me?'. Don't. Just because it's in your head doesn't mean you have to say it.
I thought we had an agreement, she thinks. *This* was not supposed to happen.
I was wrong. You don't have power over her anymore. You shouldn't have said that. I am wrong about everything. You should go on without me.

I'm talking about your zero tolerance policy toward *the mania of Narco*! You're in the *right* there. Powerful stance. Would you like to take it up a notch? Would you like to become an anti-narcomania zealot?
If *any* of them had leadership capacity beside Titus, it's him. Keep encouraging him.
If the lieutenant were an *evil* man, who knows what sort of havoc he could wreak?
If this act was about him trying to establish dominance over you, it's safe to say things didn't go as he'd planned.
Ignore him. You are addressing the entire group now.
In all truth, violence offers no answer... It's only the last available resort when all mediation fails. And here, all did fail.
Insufficiently deferential!
Is he actually comparing you -- an officer of the law -- to some neighbourhood vigilantes?!
Is he?
Is she implying the Hardie boys are THE LAW?!
It *would* be the honourable thing to do. It would really show them. Show her too.
It doesn't matter -- you're a police officer, you can do anything you want.
It is an honour to serve with you, Honour Cop!
It is. It is well within her authority. It's been clear for some time that this woman is more than she lets on.
It might be because you have the higher rank.
It seems he is leaving you no choice in the matter.
It's a bit *girly* right now. Fit it with some proper off-road components.
It's a good line, but your voice slips on *commander*, producing a little squeak. Sounds like you're afraid.
It's a shame. Recognition from an actual police officer would probably mean a lot to her. Too bad she'll never know...
It's just like back in school. Insults are weapons for bullies. Disarm him, make his weapons your own. Tell him you *do* wanna fuck him.
It's not an actual question.
It's not going well. You have to present something now. Something sane and forceful. Make your case.
It's not going well. You have to present something sane and forceful. Make your case. You've done stuff. Good stuff.
It's not the worst indignity you've suffered the last several days, but it is up there.
It's safe to assume this is their leader. Or at least he thinks he is.
It's safe to say he *did not* establish dominance over you.
It's safe to say he has established dominance over you.
It's still a point for you. No denying it.
It's true. And let's not bring up that shameful oinking episode.
Jungles, villages, mud... he better not be wasting your time.
Just like there are no *honour points*. None whatsoever.
Khm... Correction: Lieutenant Double-Yefreitor Love.
Kim is never going to be okay with you handing something like this off to a minor. This plan is either off the table or you need to do it when he's not around.
Kingsman? Yes! Please, let's be that.
Leadership -- now.
Let's never bring up that shameful episode again.
Let's show him: Raise your brow high, up to high-noon.
Like a general over his maps, he plots his moves. Judging by the way he's licking his chops, it's going to be a good one.
Listen to him. This is a man who clearly knows about bodies and fridges -- and bodies fitting into fridges.
Living in the name of virtue and doing noble deeds while expecting nothing in return is not *archaic*. How can you even think that?
Lots of feeling. Feel it up, *way* up.
MORE.
Make her meet your eyes.
Makes sense. Friends are nice on Sunday. You don't have to work, you can just spend time with pals, watching rugby and drinking beer.
Martyrs of the Nation, the ardent knights. The few who had the guts to do this.
Me? Get outta here, I'm solid.
Meaningless?! Vulgar knave, how dare you besmirch the honour of honour points... It hurts the eyes to look upon filth like you. Begone.
Men of duty. Dark duty.
Mewling wimp! Pathetic...
Mhmh-mhmh, you are like a wondering knight-monk setting right the wrongs of the world you come upon.
More like a slave to any master who tosses a bone your way.
More.
Most dishonourable are those who have known honour, carried honour in their hearts, and then disowned honour. They are the *honourfallen*...
Murderers. Twisted by orders. Young boys forced into killing.
Never. You're no sorry cop. *He* should apologise to *you*.
No -- It's your soul, your blue soul.
No one speaks to a police officer in that tone. Show the little missy here her place.
No one? There's something about her... She's definitely not a 'no one'.
No! Do *not* keep this random civilian in any loops.
No! Do *not* report back to this random civilian about anything.
No! You don't *need* to ever talk to these little shitheads again. It's much more important to prove to this kid you're a man. A *big* man.
No, it's not.
No, no, no! You see, you're putting the needs of *others* above *yours*. *You* get nothing in return -- not even praise. You always get the short, *noble* end of the stick...
No. Alcohol is a powerful anti-narco agent. Seventy-eight percent of people who use alcohol daily feel no need to use it's opposite -- narcohol. If anything, you should drink more. It cleanses.
No. By supporting the *traditional* tobacco industry, you keep the narco mills in check. If anything, you should smoke *more*. Tobacco smoke also wards off narco spirits.
No. Stop goofing around, recruit!
No.
Nor should you. Nudity is *shameful*. No officer of the law should ever be caught *sans pantalones*.
Not honourable, recruit!! Not honourable at all.
Not quite there yet -- push on.
Not to mention the airborne artillery platform she works on. Her every word is backed up by the most powerful ordnance available...
Not to mention the damage that would be dealt to the force's reputation by the corpse just rotting here like this...
Not you, you would like to say... But then there's that weasel door.
Nothing but you, a detective of the RCM, standing there with your mouth agape.
Nothing to see here. Just a regular law official reacting to every day stimuli. Nothing noteworthy here.
Now it's not a big deal for him anymore. This is how he saves face.
Now that *is* interesting.
Now's your chance. Show the lieutenant what happens when he patronizes you.
Now, counter his counter! Brow to the troposphere! Full hoist!
Now. Push him now.
OH YEAH, FEEL THE POWER.
ON THE SCENE
Obviously. Look at that vague yet threatening stuff spewing forth from your mouth...
Odd. Something about the way she says it makes you think she's not bothered by any of the cop-show questioning.
Of course not. You've always been anti-narco militant material. You kept *her* off a *dark* course with that interjection! Are you ready to *save lives*?
Of course there were honour points. Did you think no one was paying attention to all of your honourable deeds, Honourable One?
Of course, you're turning over a dangerous LAWBREAKER.
Of course. No one puts words in *your* mouth.
Of course. You're a cop, you were *born* to do this stuff.
Officer? Even after a rude awakening -- this dockworker respects the police. More than they usually do at least.
Oh god, the lieutenant is here too! Do not DISHONOUR the force!
Oh, I bet he's still fuming internally due to your *no-sitting* escapades. He doesn't like to lose a battle of will.
Oh, the modesty on this one. Another 10 honour points for you, sir!
Okay, this time... is FOR REAL. You have *two* bad options ruled out -- the odds are on your side!
One leader and one follower, the most ancient power dynamic.
Overpriced mercenaries putting down some rabid dogs. Someone's gotta do it, right?
People will be in awe, admiring a police officer most honourable in a sacred ritual of uttermost righteousness.

Pipe down Puzzle Face, this is a Code Red situation! If we review all the *bad words*, that's only going to add more temptation to saying them -- in front of the *children*. And we *do not* want that.
Please don't set up a mass execution. You'll never be able to pull it off. The logistics alone are insane... Just ask questions. Be a normal cop.
Please stop hanging around the jungle-gym. Whatever you're doing there is making you look awful. And it's not helping you with these guys at all.
Push her *further*. Show her the error of her ways.
Questions will show him who's boss.
Quit while you're ahead? Or... no?
Real men, real politics, real thoughts in your head!
Retreat is not a winning tactic.
Right. Puzzle Face shouldn't be the cool guy when he fails. *I* was supposed to be that.
SAY IT. You know you want to.
Say it's *classified*. An old soldier should understand.
See? He's being evasive. Shake him up, show him who's boss.
Selflessness forms the basis for the warrior code. An ageless code, as fit for nomadic Iilmaraan tribes traversing the vast Red Plateau in Provincia Gurdi as it is for your neighbourhood postman.
Semenese Race Scientists?
She acknowledges you as her equal.
She appreciates your effort to curb the deviantry and laxness.
She only gave it to you because she doesn't see you as competition.
She refuses?! But your word is the *law*. *You* are LAW!
She shut you down -- because you don't have enough backbone.
She sounds almost disappointed with you. *Reprimanding* you for falling and hurting your knee.
She speaks with the authority of a leader. Hers is the final word around here.
She stops mid-sentence. That's it -- you got him. He's going to give it up, but on his terms.
She's got a couple of ranks herself. Honoured chief and so on.
She's having a good time arguing against the law. Too good, perhaps?
She's probably not gonna do it.
She's proud of her organizational abilities even now -- and trying to raise that pride as a bulwark against the desperation.
She's refusing to adopt the demeanour of a cornered animal. A leader with no one to lead -- she still wants to retain some control of the situation.
She's right -- when was the last time? You should get an un-androgynous crew cut.
She's right, showing up to work on time is important.
Show him the ham still got it!
Shut *down*.
Slap the cuffs on her!
So she says she didn't do it -- and she doesn't trust you. Is it you specifically or the Citizens Militia that she distrusts?
So unprofessional of you... Pity!
Soldiers... people in a bad spot they probably did not choose, ordered to commit acts they wanted no part of.
Something about the way she says it makes you think she's not bothered by any of the cop-show questioning.
Something cracks in the lieutenant. He's letting his pride get in the way.
Something else is in charge now.
Something that proves you haven't completely lost it. Something *solid*. You've *done* things. Good things. List those.
Something that proves you haven't lost your mind.
Sounds like *Isobel* is the leader around here.
Sounds like usual aggressive posturing.
Staff issues, always tough on the leadership. You smell traces of betrayal.
Stop embarrassing yourself. Pop a magnesium and calm down.
Success. Titus backs off. Fists down, everybody.
Take heed of these wise words. *You're* in charge. 'Sorry' is a beggar's plea reserved for those beneath you.
Take it! You're a police officer. Police officers carry guns.
Take the advice.
Take the documents off him and you take his power.
Technicalities.
Technically, possession of narcotics is legal in Revachol. But you should still reprimand her.
That certainly *sounds* like a certain *delinquent youth* who likes to harass you from her balcony.
That child's a common delinquent, not an artist. He probably can't even hold a paint brush.
That didn't work at all, he's *more* focused now.
That didn't work at all.
That eyebrow! He's using his Authority against you... you... can't resist...
That feels like a jab, but honestly -- you deserve it. Plus, it would be *rude* to reprimand her.
That means you should do it whenever you feel like someone's disrespecting your authority.
That was not the right move. You should have pushed on.
That would mean you're openly showing the people that you're taking the Union's side.
That's *not* a problem. At all. The more drugs you do, the more zealous you can be in combating them. Drugs give you the *vigour* to fight drugs.
That's all he's going to say on this subject.
That's cop for: "I haven't offered to pay because I don't have any money either."
That's him *allowing* the police to ask his men questions.
That's it. There's some kind of power issue they don't want to admit -- and the missing Hardie is involved.
That's just Dick Mullen's *modus operandi*. He might bend the rules, but he closes cases no one else can.
That's only because you haven't *really* laid down the law with her yet.
That's right! Way to put them in the little boys corner!
That's right. Now skip the foreplay. Time to dive into the dark alleys, start shaking down the usual suspects -- you know, *legwork*.
That's right. You don't *owe* him any explanation.
That's right. You'll make your own luck.
That's still a lie! RE-ASSERT YOURSELF.
That... was the most honourable thing *anyone* has ever done. You are virtue embodied.
That... was the most honourable thing anyone has ever done, Harry.
That... was the most honourable thing anyone has ever done.
The authority of The Law is clearly unquestionable.
The bonds between officers -- partners -- are *paramount*. That's how trust is built. Trust saves lives.
The call was a courtesy. He must have held her in high regard to personally tell her he knew about her plan to run drugs for the competition.
The final word on this matter has been spoken.
The first man to break formation is always a blow to leadership. This is bigger than he lets on.
The glasses? Forget about the glasses. It's his attitude.
The hole in this fence doesn't look *that* huge.
The kids are overwhelming you. Regain control of yourself and the situation.
The leader of a massive on-air game built by these people.
The lieutenant is a narcomaniac!
The lieutenant is a noble man, but he does not *know* honour like you and I do. Best not to waste time trying to explain. Lead by example.
The lieutenant is about to interject. Cut him off. This decision should be yours.
The lieutenant is not particularly interested in this information. He's just trying to assert some control over the conversation.
The lieutenant's command instantly lets all the wind out of your sails, like he overrode your mind or flicked some secret master-switch.
The lieutenant's impassive mask has been replaced with intensity. He speaks not as a cop, but as a citizen. He is *Vacholiere*. A Revacholian.
The lieutenant's voice betrays a slight disappointment, which he glosses over by reasserting control.
The long and short of it is: you're his superior.
The sage will not budge on this.
The sentence has been passed.
The virtue you exhibit in even the most difficult of moral dilemmas, speaks of the purest of heart and soul. Soon it will be time for another Arch.
The whole charade was about establishing dominance over you. It's safe to say he has succeeded.
The whole charade was about establishing dominance over you. You backing up means he's succeeded.
The word lingers in the air of basement, far away ice cream makers are buzzing and the sea wind blows outside...
The word lingers in the air of the basement, far away ice cream makers are buzzing and the sea wind blows outside...
The word lingers in the air of the yard, as far away dogs are barking, further yet the sound of motor traffic...
The word lingers in the air of the yard, far away dogs are barking, further yet the sound of motor traffic...
The words "I *am* an enemy commander" sound cold as iron from your lungs. The old man still hugs his gun...
The words leave your mouth as if walking up the stairs -- everything is going fine until you reach the end and take one step too many:
The words nail the Cafeteria Manager to his place. Unmoving he stares down at the counter with his cheeks reddish.
The words nail the man to his place. Unmoving, he stares down at the counter with his cheeks reddish.
Then they will witness honour so great it seems nigh impossible a single man can contain it. They will admire you, Honourable One.
Then we're *definitely* doing it.
Then why are you wasting time with *her*? You should demand to speak with who's *really* in charge.
Then you have nothing more to say here.
There is *plenty* here to work with once he's in custody -- proceed with a sure hand now!
There is a silence around this man's words -- unlike Titus, they're *afraid* of him. That's the type of respect he commands.
There is an acrimonious note. It's clear he's doing the real work around here.
There is little left to salvage... Did I not tell you to pick the *right* option?
There is little you can do to help her now, but given the chance you feel like you *should*. There is something about her. A weightiness.
There is no 'back'.
There is no other officer on the scene, only you. You *have* to get a hold of yourself -- finish the interrogation.
There is no surprise in his voice. Only careless superiority.
There is no talking him out of this -- and no, the incident has not had a good effect on his opinion of you either.
There is no train. Just you and the cold light of truth.
There must be *some* friction there. He's keeping it well hidden, however...
There will be no *spinning*. Do the *honest* thing! Pull the trigger!
There won't be a next season. Not for this. Find the phasmid or admit defeat, people.
There! Working class drunk! You know what this means, right?
There's a bit of truth in there for Titus to react so brashly. He does not like his authority questioned.
There's a hint of defensiveness to her retort, but also confidence. She seems to sit up a little straighter in her chair.
There's a slightly weary, condescending note in her voice. She doesn't trust your professionalism following your rather tasteless come-ons.
There's always your backup outside... stay firm. You've got the upper hand.
There's no need for champagne when there's honour, recruit. Go and tell the working class woman what you found *right now*.
There's only one solution to this. You're a businessman.
There, you've breached his defenses. His secrets are yours.
There. No one is saying anything. The silence agrees.
These *skull* people are more than an authority. They're deities.
These three stripes give you strength in this dangerous realm. It would be foolish to remove them.
They keep speaking for Titus so that he wouldn't get his hands muddy with too much explaining.
They pay you no heed or pretend not to notice you.
They would have to. To project strength and power.
They're playing to their *considerable* strength in numbers. Don't talk about arresting them. You'll only bring attention to your inability to do so.
Things were going so well before. There's no other option. The process must be interrupted.
This Carly is a made up person. It's nothing but a mean joke. They just want to see how you wriggle out of it.
This Ruby, in her phrasing, is entrusted with great power. She trusts her. So do the others.
This battle is not going to be worth your while. Let little miss sass here be.
This citizen thinks she can do without your assistance? Don't buy it. They all need help.
This feels *honourable*. You did the *right* thing not taking the medal. The *good* thing. Your chest is buzzing with pride.
This guy will be the first one to bail.
This guy's got Authority off the charts. With just a flick of his eyebrow he's able to make you his thrall.
This has gone far enough. You're going to show dignity toward your fellow officer and *apologize*.
This is a slippery one -- he may respect the law, but mostly wants to pass under the radar. You won't get a chance to talk after you walk out of here.
This is big. It's as big of a thank-you as Titus Hardie can muster under *any* circumstances.
This is for your posse. They got to keep it up. They got to move. You are the major mic enforcer.
This is it. Judgement time!
This is it... you're finally getting to speak with those who hold *real* power.
This is not what he thinks it is -- this is a great idea.
This is slander. The king was just and beautiful. The revolutionaries made this all up.
This is the face worn by the first law-bringers, the noble tyrants. Today you join their number...
This is the look of a man who's *defeated*. He knows he's out of excuses.
This is the only opinion he cares for.
This is where Cuno establishes dominance. Over you.
This is years of experience talking. He's seen career after career ruined by careless media strategies.
This is your chance to impress the lieutenant!
This line of questioning is going nowhere! Try harder!
This man is truly sorry for underestimating the honour and military might of the Seolite man.
This seems serious...
This sounds like a seditious fantasy! How can they allow books like this in print?
This time it's going to work. You already ruled out the first, *bad* option that didn't.
This would *show* them all. We need to get you on that list of discoverers, no question about that.
Throwing rocks at motor carriages isn't really the same thing as running a drug lab, is it?
Time to arrest him!
Titus doesn't reply -- probably a good sign.
Titus lets the others clear out the details -- so he doesn't get his hands muddy with explaining too much.
Titus. Keep addressing him. He *wants* everyone to know he's in charge.
To punish her.
To redeem yourself before the hoods of Law. Now start asking questions, like a real law enforcement official.
Too late. The die is cast. This is street-style brow-fighting. Two of a kind but one won't survive.
Towering over her in your insignia, you make her feel safe.
Trying to recruit him is like trying to make a pet out of fire. This kid recognizes only one authority -- his own.
Two! One! Do it, get this *one* thing right.
Use this momentary confusion to take *control* of the situation.
VICTORY!
Vigilantes? You're a professional officer of the only *legitimate* authority in Revachol.
WHAT?!
Wait, did he say you don't have *big guns*?
Was it a good idea on the lieutenant's part to get into this? Don't even answer that, just leave. You can always come back later -- on your own terms.
We'll take care of that body too. We'll take care of all of it!
We're not off to a good start. This missing gun business is making your hand tremble.
Well... The rest of that nancy stuff was really just there to make the gun thing more appealing. But it might work anyway.
Weren't you warned to *not* go down this path?! And yet you *still* go and do it...
What *does* he do then? This feels like a good opportunity to DOMINATE him.
What a proud warrior! This is how the hunted becomes the hunter in this dark dangerous place.
What a shitty gun. But it's better than nothing.
What delicious power hid within this pathetic mess... you feel better.
What did she just say? That's not how a civilian is supposed to address an officer of the law.
What do you mean *what did I crack*? Look at how working class that drunk is!
What does *he* have to say for *himself*? He left you to catch the bullets!
What gall he had to admonish you earlier for your prejudice, yet here he is, saying cops are usually dull-minded. Are you going to let him get away with being a hypocrite?

What happened? Did your balls shrivel up? You had plenty of chances to get off this road, but no, you let this guy keep talking. Then when the time came to make your choice you tried to split the difference. Weak.
What happened? You got your ass handed to you. Always go with the gun! How are you going to get out of this now?
What happened? You got your ass handed to you. You should have made the gun work! How are you going to get out of this now?
What is implied here?! That you're a *drunk*?!
What is that patronizing tone? Someone needs to put the lieutenant in his place.
What is that patronizing tone? You're one of the suzerain's trusted administrators! You may need to put the lieutenant in his place...
What is this insolence? She misunderstood.
What is this? Destruction of property? Hooliganism? Elder abuse? And who is this *scamp*, anyway?
What is this? Destruction of property? Hooliganism? Elder abuse?
What is wrong with you? Now you're just empowering these punks.
What would these guys have to offer to any organization?
What's going on, are you a racist now? Is the rally real? Please don't set up any rally, it'll make you look awful!
What's going on? It's like you're a puppet in his hands.
What's harder core than a little bit of...
What? Are you intimidated by this sissy *bureaucrat*? Mediocre!
What?! You can't compare the sidearm issued to you personally by the RCM to the pistol of a known criminal?
Whatever you do, do not admit to these punks you lost your gun. You will suffer if you do.
When power calls you, you come. But power itself is a fragile trick of perception.
When you hold something up long enough, some people are compelled to take it. Try it on the lieutenant.
When you meet this *Titus*, tell him about this. See what he has to say.
Who cares about sexual partners and their promiscuity when you've got *the fate and honour of Revachol* on your shoulders?
Who's Dick Mullen now?
Who's the one in charge? You are!
Why don't you take out your trusty Villiers instead?
Wow. Turned the lieutenant off like he was a busted old radio. He really is the *lord* of his realm.
Yeah, I think she got it. Good job!
Yeah, maybe you're right... it works better if it's not a kid at the other end of the barrel.
Yeah. Sure.
Yes, take it... and then dispose of it! This den of narcomania cannot be allowed stand!
Yes, you. He's talking about you, you grovelling sycophant.
Yes. And you found him. Now go and tell the working class woman. Protect and serve, recruit!
Yes. Make it clear you're the one setting the terms here...
Yes. You're pure. Unravaged by Narco. Of course it's pretty obvious you've done a lot of drugs in the past -- and will probably do more in the future -- but that doesn't matter.
Yes. Your deeds have spoken for you, Honourable One, but to fully drape one's self in an eternal cloak of honour a ritual must be completed. A *Rite of Honour*...
You *get* it.
You absolute fool.
You acted with great compassion and dignity, not taking advantage of this man. Praise thine honourable heart!
You already tried and all you got was *hurt*. Better not to try anymore. Then you won't get HURT.
You are *THE LAW*. He must divulge the information!
You are an officer of the law with way more important things to worry about than *boules*. Don't take this crap.
You are not a *fool*.
You are on the fast track to becoming one of the rarest, most revered of all police officers -- an honour cop.
You believe the place was called *Precinct 41* -- it was also filled with (almost exclusively) *men*, sitting on desks, talking shit and wasting time.
You brought much shame on your family today. Your name will forever be tarnished by this most shameful of deeds. Be forever banished, Shameful One!
You can feel your cheeks turn red under the lieutenant's gaze -- you look like a kid, not a police officer. Even the war-paint can't conceal the embarrassment.
You can now go and tell Titus about this. See what he has to say.
You can't get off now.
You can't think of anything convincing. Time for some police brutality!
You caught her off guard. Push her some more.
You could ask him to show you the soles of his boots. He definitely looks like someone capable of a lynching. Maybe he was present?
You could push him by asking him to show you the soles of his boots. He definitely looks like someone capable of a lynching.
You don't *feel* scary? What are you, some kind of *diva*?
You don't have a *style*, you have a *duty*. Now start asking questions, like a real law enforcement official.
You don't have to be the butt of every joke. You're making the lieutenant look bad. Just stick to what you know. Guns.
You don't have to be the butt of every joke. You're making the lieutenant look bad.
You don't have to tell her you met Evrart *right away*. There's time.
You don't need anybody else. You're the *lawbringer*...
You don't need no stinkin' committee. You're the Law.
You don't shush me! He could say he was taking the fall for someone. Or disoriented. A confession is useless without a motive, don't mess this up.
You feel like you earned a significant amount of honour points today.
You got shredded to pieces there! Should've gone with the gun, what were you thinking?!
You have *so* many bad options ruled out it's *insane*! There is no way in the world you won't do it this time!
You have little reason to doubt that's her real name, but... she's not all that surprised by this cop-show questioning, is she?
You have more control than they do, the agreement should reflect that. You should push for 60%.
You have sworn a holy oath Harry. She herself begged you to not let her go.
You hear that? Someone's *missing*. There could be *foul play* afoot!
You made the right call there. It feels good. Pure.
You need to address that remark.
You need to bring the thumb of your right hand -- your sword hand -- to your rectum and stick it in there to form the Arch of Honours.
You need to save face if you want her respect. DEMAND that passport!
You say the words loudly to yourself. That makes them very convincing. The world applauds you -- you are finally on the holy warpath against narcomania!
You set the pace and the topic of these conversations here. Establish that.
You should begin by interrogating those lawless malcontents at the Dockworkers' Union. They're an obvious place to start.
You shouldn't be seeing him in an intimate setting. For some reason you feel this man is your... *superior*.
You stepped on some big toes there. Tried to take out a satellite. That's not how it's going to go. They'd rather rot in jail than implicate Mr. Claire in this mess.
You were specifically asked *not* to say that -- and then you said it anyway. You junior delinquent! But you *still* did!
You would look nice as a statue...
You'll be indebted to her, in a way. But one step ahead of the Union in another...
You're a police officer and a *grown up*. Why are you trying to impress her? Maybe try a different approach? Like climbing up there...
You're a police officer and a *grown up*. Why are you trying to impress her? Maybe try a different approach?
You're pretty sure you've had at least two years of *cop school* and many more of active service.
You're right. The lieutenant, although a noble man, does not understand honour like you do. Best not to waste time trying to explain. Lead by example.
You're saying it really loud, but it's not coming out right. Maybe add more indignation?
You're wearing the vestments of *order* and *hierarchy*. You have nothing to answer for.
You're well versed in the kind of threatening legalese that implies criminal liability but in fact has no meaning whatsoever.
You've earned his respect. See how you've *marked* him?
You've really moved up in his estimation.
Your cop training did not prepare you for this. What to do?
Your eyes are fixed on her as everything gets very still -- dangerously so...
Your words sound hollow and unconvincing. You're a weakling, a servant to the dark arts of narcomania.
"NO," you say, and hand out your firearm to him. Your hand trembles and your eyes are full of fear.
"No," you scream: "BEHIND YOU!" from your bloody lips. Your eyes are full of fear.
"Shoot... dead." You hear your bloody lips mumble. You try to give him your gun, so he'd understand. But he does not. He does not look into your eyes and see the fear there.
"There... behind..." You hear your bloody lips mumble, but he does not understand. He does not look into your eyes and see the fear there. He does not trust you.
